description: Pupil answer hits both RAM mark points — expect full marks.

part.raw_answer: RAM is volatile, meaning it loses its contents when the computer is switched off. It stores data and instructions that the CPU is currently using.

markPoints.text: Volatile — loses contents when power is removed
accepted_alternatives: loses its data when switched off

markPoints.text: Stores data/instructions currently in use by the CPU

questionStem: A computer has RAM and a hard disk.
modelAnswer: RAM is volatile memory that loses its contents when power is removed. It stores the data and instructions the CPU is currently using.
